### **Executive Summary: Comparative Analysis of RF-Based Aesthetic Modalities**

This document provides a comparative efficacy and safety analysis of three radiofrequency (RF) treatments: **Gold Microneedling RF**, **Monopolar RF (X-Century)**, and **SMBS (XS)**. The primary distinction lies in the delivery mechanism and its impact on treatment precision, depth control, and risk profile. Gold Microneedling RF demonstrates superior precision and safety regarding skin surface integrity and fat preservation, while Monopolar RF carries higher risks of unpredictable depth and fat atrophy.

---

### **1. Clinical Parameters & Patient Experience**

| Parameter | Gold Microneedling RF | Monopolar RF (X-Century) | SMBS (XS) |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **Recommended Sessions** | 2–3 sessions (per area) | 1 session | 1–2 sessions
